items.title: Tino Livramento undergoes surgery after being forced to leave England’s World Cup squad
items.summary: TINO LIVRAMENTO has successfully undergone surgery after picking up an injury on World Cup duty. The Newcastle full-back was forced to withdraw from the England squad just days before their opener against Croatia. items.title: Spurs agree £85m deal for West Ham's Fernandes ahead of Man Utd
items.summary: Tottenham have agreed a deal to sign Mateus Fernandes from West Ham.

items.title: Tottenham WIN staggering £85million Mateus Fernandes transfer race with Man Utd not willing to pay huge price tag
items.summary: TOTTENHAM have beaten Manchester United to the signing of Mateus Fernandes from West Ham. Spurs are believed to have been willing to go to a higher financial level than United to sign the Portugal midfielder. items.summary: SOUTH LONDON'S finest will face off against each other when England’s Djed Spence takes on Aaron Wan-Bissaka’s DR Congo in Atlanta on Wednesday. items.title: Everton close in on deals for Tyrique George and Hayden Hackney
items.summary: George played for club on loan from Chelsea last season Middlesbrough’s Hackney expected to have medical Everton are close to agreeing a fee with Chelsea for the winger Tyrique George as Hayden Hackney puts the finishing touches to his move from Middlesbrough. items.title: Jurgen Klopp refuses to rule out return to management during World Cup TV appearance
items.summary: JURGEN KLOPP refused to rule out replacing Julian Nagelsmann as Germany coach after Die Mannschaft were sent packing from the World Cup on penalties. items.title: Meet Sweden World Cup wags led by Arsenal star’s actress girlfriend and Tottenham ace’s high school sweetheart
items.summary: SWEDEN could be out of the World Cup by the end of tonight, but they arrived at the World Cup with one of the most glamorous Wag squads in football. items.title: Australia thrash West Indies to reach T20 World Cup semi-final LIVE!
Brook: England Test captaincy would be 'great honour'
items.summary: Harry Brook has said it would be a "great honour" to take on the captaincy of the England Test side after Ben Stokes' abrupt retirement from international cricket.

items.title: Australia thrash West Indies to race into Women's T20 World Cup final
items.summary: Australia breezed into the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup final with a convincing eight-wicket win over the West Indies at The Oval.

items.title: Archer, Tongue rested as England pick Wood, Mahmood for T20 opener vs India
items.summary: England bowlers Jofra Archer and Josh Tongue have been rested for England's T20 international against India in Durham, with Luke Wood and Saqib Mahmood drafted in for the series-opener.

items.title: AC Milan sign PSG's Portugal striker Ramos for £60m
items.summary: AC Milan sign Paris St-Germain striker Goncalo Ramos for a reported club record transfer fee of about £60m.

items.title: Coach played mind games with Cusack before death, inquest told
items.summary: A team-mate tells an inquest Maddy Cusack was "anxious" about Jonathan Morgan joining Sheffield United.

items.title: Could set-piece coach be Celtic's key signing?
items.summary: BBC Scotland crunches the numbers behind Ross Grant's set-piece impact at Hearts and looks at what he could bring to Celtic.
